Ehh, this is a dubious narrative. Twitter is down 17% from right before when Elon bought a stake on April 4, causing the stock price to spike. But it's been a horrible period for tech stocks and the NASDAQ is down 20% in the same period. https://www.nytimes.com/... https://twitter.com/...

I feel like the people who believe the self-driving Tesla crashing into the clown car will destroy it are underestimating how well the clown car has held up to disaster historically. Twitter is an Internet Volvo. We will all ride in it come hell or high Elon. It's that good. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

State of play: 1/ general legal consensus is that Twitter has upper hand on Elon, based on what's currently known. 2/ Twitter will look even stronger after it files complaint, because plaintiffs almost always do. 3/ real intrigue begins when Elon (via his lawyers) reply.
Twitter's lawyers get their turn: “...Mr. Musk and the other Musk Parties have knowingly, intentionally, willfully, and materially breached the Agreement...” https://twitter.com/...

In a new letter, Twitter's lawyers respond to Musk's lawyers saying, essentially, 1.) no the deal is not terminated, 2.) your client has not suffered a material adverse effect, and 3.) we will do what is necessary (i.e. sue) in order to complete the deal. https://www.sec.gov/...

In an internal post, Meta Vice President of Remote Presence and Engineering Maher Saba instructed managers to identify and “move to exit” poor performers
In leaked memo, Facebook tells managers low performers don't belong
‘They Are Not Who We Need:’ Meta Reportedly Advising Managers to Axe Poor Performing Employees
Meta exec tells managers to ‘exit’ workers who are ‘coasting’ and ‘failing’
Meta Managers Are Urged to Identify, Push Out Low Performers
Low performers are in the cross hairs as Facebook's parent braces for tough times
Meta reportedly tells managers to identify people for layoffs
Facebook engineering head tells managers to find low performers, those that are ‘failing this company,’ so they can be let go

Here's the latest happening at Meta: “Identify people who need support early, partner with our Employee Relations team to get that support, and move to exit people who are unable to get on track.” https://www.wsj.com/... with @JeffHorwitz

I don't know what to say other than this is a horrible spot to put managers and employees in. I get needing to rein in costs but there's a better way to do so with more empathy and support than this. https://twitter.com/...
Meta is looking to get low performers or coasters out of there, I can see more companies looking to do this https://twitter.com/...

Looking forward to all those think pieces about the weaponized PIP. A lot of people are going to come out of this traumatized. FB has plenty of money and should just do layoffs fair and square. but I am under no illusions they will ever do the right thing.

Facebook tells managers to identify low performers in memo “If a direct report is coasting or is a low performer, they are not who we need; they are failing this company” https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

Loved my time at Meta & vast majority of the ppl there are super talented. But I hope increased performance scrutiny makes its way up to the VP level as well. In my experience the small # of low performers aren't exclusively clustered around ICs https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

Facebook told engineers managers to report low performers into its HR system by 5pm today: “As a manager, you cannot allow someone to be net neutral or negative for Meta.” Ah the cruelty of corporatespeak https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

“If a direct report is coasting or a low performer, they are not who we need; they are failing this company,” Mr. Saba wrote. “As a manager, you cannot allow someone to be net neutral or negative for Meta.” https://www.wsj.com/...

@sal19 @JeffHorwitz This runs contrary to what I've heard from software engineers over the course of the past few years. Many people chose to work there because of the shortage of accountability. The pressure on recruiters and managers to fill open roles was everything the company measured on.
Turns out merit matters over DEI in Big Tech? Facebook tells managers to identify low performers in memo - The Washington Post https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

The head of engineering at Meta has instructed his managers to identify low performers with the intent of weeding them out. @sal19 ⁦@JeffHorwitz⁩ on latest sign of belt-tightening at the social-media giant. https://www.wsj.com/...

New from me: A Meta exec told managers in a Workplace group to identify employees by 5pm today who “need support,” and to “move to exit people who are unable to get on track,” according to an internal post. https://www.theinformation.com/ ...
This isn't performance management, it is forced cutting. https://twitter.com/...
I don't think there's a clearer sign of a recession than this https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
Interesting to see Meta now blame its failings on its employees as it urges teams to report “low performers.” “They are failing this company,” Mr. Saba wrote. https://twitter.com/...
The FTC says it will crack down on tech companies' illegal use and sharing of highly sensitive data and false claims about data-anonymization
Location, health, and other sensitive information: FTC committed to fully enforcing the law against illegal use and sharing of highly sensitive data
FTC warns tech companies against misusing health data, following Biden's executive order protecting abortion access
FTC says it will crack down on medical and location data sharing after abortion privacy concerns
FTC says it will pursue companies that exploit location and health data
FTC will boost efforts to prevent sharing of sensitive data amid abortion concerns

In post about sensitive health and location data, @FTC warns claims about data being anonymous or anonymized are “often deceptive.” “Companies that make false claims about anonymization can expect to hear from the FTC.”  https://www.ftc.gov/... https://twitter.com/...

FTC says it will finally crack down on privacy-violating companies that use claims of “anonymization” as some kind of get out of free card, since multiple studies have shown the terms to be gibberish: https://twitter.com/...

Seems like a pretty clear warning shot to tech companies that the FTC isn't going to wait on federal privacy legislation or a rulemaking process to go hard on illicit data sharing. https://www.ftc.gov/... https://twitter.com/...
FTC declares that anonymizing data isn't sufficient for protecting consumer privacy. https://twitter.com/...

https://www.ftc.gov/... “Significant research has shown that “anonymized” data can often be re-identified, especially in the context of location data...Companies that make false claims about anonymization can expect to hear from the FTC”

While this is not entirely unexpected, it is bad news for marketers. Now it will fall to marketers to ensure that data they are using in their engagement solutions is “sufficiently” anonymized to meet a somewhat undefined FTC standard for anonymization. How do we manage at scale? https://twitter.com/...

The @FTC said in a blog post today that the combination of #location data and #health data collected by apps “creates a new frontier of potential harms to consumers” on #privacy: https://www.ftc.gov/...

After Biden issues executive order urging FTC to take steps to protect reproductive health data, agency says in blog post it is “committed to fully enforcing the law against illegal use and sharing of highly sensitive data” https://www.ftc.gov/...

New on the Business Blog: Location, health, and other sensitive information: FTC committed to fully enforcing the law against illegal use and sharing of highly sensitive data. More: https://www.ftc.gov/... #privacy #DataSecurity

.@FTC: “highly personal information that people choose not to disclose [...] is actually shared with complete strangers [in] the often shadowy ad tech & data broker ecosystem where companies have a profit motive to share data at an unprecedented scale...” https://www.ftc.gov/...

European regulators moving faster than the media in moving TikTok from the exciting underdog bucket to untrustworthy giant corporation list. The inevitable pendulum swing spares no one. https://techcrunch.com/...
BigScience, an effort by 1K+ researchers and NLP startup Hugging Face, releases BLOOM, an open-access multilingual large language model bigger than GPT-3
A year in the making, BigScience's AI language model is finally available
AI goes multilingual with Hugging Face's BLOOM

There's also a political angle: BLOOM was trained on a French supercomputer. This is a big political win for France the EU, who are super keen to boost their “digital sovereignty” ie build cutting-edge technology in the EU. ,https://www.technologyreview.com/ ...

Meet BLOOM — a large language model that works in 46 natural languages built by +1k AI researchers from around the world. Its creators are doing something pretty radical in AI research: putting all their work out in the open and letting anyone access it. https://www.technologyreview.com/ ...

Inside a radical new project to democratize AI A group of over 1,000 AI researchers has created a multilingual large language model bigger than GPT-3—and they're giving it out for free. https://www.technologyreview.com/ ... @techreview @BigScienceLLM

Sources: China decided to rein in Tencent after two scandals, including a secret WeChat project to use data to predict new Politburo standing committee members
Adapted from Influence Empire: The Story of Tencent and China's Tech Ambition by Lulu Chen.  Seems like this would be a great read! https://twitter.com/...
One takeaway is that even vice-minister level Chinese politicians can have almost no idea who will end up on the politburo standing committee https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...
“Beijing learned of a project inside the company designed to predict political succession...” This sounds like a good book from @luluyilun https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...
How China's WeChat became so powerful it could have posed a real threat to Beijing's rule https://www.bloomberg.com/... via @BW

Why is Beijing wary of Tencent's influence? Ex vice police chief asked WeChat to feed him info about fellow politicians, and Tencent had a model predicting Politburo succession. https://www.bloomberg.com/... @luluyilun uncovered in her new book. Get a copy when it's out!

I spent the past month using iOS and iPadOS 16. Stage Manager is a major shift for iPadOS, and it's doing something I thought was impossible: making me like overlapping windows. My preview story: A New iPad Era https://www.macstories.net/... https://twitter.com/...
2) I would forgo the updated Lock Screen if that meant that iOS 16 didn't drop support for so many older devices. iPadOS didn't get an updated Lock Screen and it only dropped support for iPad Air 2.

Always a strange day when Apple releases the first public betas and all the tech sites that respect the developer beta NDA suddenly get to talk about all the new features like they are brand new haha. Nothing really new to cover from a MacRumors perspective.

The iOS 16 Lock Screen is going to be a massive hit. I've had so much fun playing with wallpapers, Photo Shuffle, fonts, and colors. Customization is back after 2 years. And that's before *the* big feature of it is even available: third-party widgets. https://www.macstories.net/... https://twitter.com/...
I want iOS 16 asap just for the new lockscreen setup https://twitter.com/...

How AI could help make Wikipedia entries more accurate https://tech.fb.com/...

A new AI model produced by the @MetaAI team will improve the quality of Wikipedia entries by checking citations against over 134 million web ages stored in a new library called Sphere. https://techmonitor.ai/...

The last project I worked on at FAIR London is out 🚀🎉 We build an AI system which helps editors make Wikipedia citations better. The architecture relies on Sphere - a web-scale, white-box retrieval engine that you can read more about here https://arxiv.org/.... Congrats team! https://twitter.com/...

Super excited to finally release our latest work where we show that AI can help humans in verifying Wikipedia claims. Everything is open-sourced: data, models, code and our new Sphere retrieval white-box engine. A step forward in general trustworthiness of information online 🚀 https://twitter.com/...

It never stops to amaze me just how great @Wikipedia is. Among many things, it has been *the* source of knowledge for many of today's AI models. Really happy that I can be part of the AI community that tries to help humans improve it! https://twitter.com/...

Really pleased this is finally released! Proud of the team effort from a bunch of folks, led by the amazing @Fabio_Petroni, and a ton stellar modelling work by @BroscheitSamuel :) https://twitter.com/...

This Thursday, July 14 at 7.30am Pacific time, I'll be joined by @MetaAI researchers @Fabio_Petroni and @riedelcastro to discuss how AI could help make Wikipedia entries more accurate: https://twitter.com/... Hope to see you there!

(2/2) As a knowledge source for our model, we created Sphere, a retrieval engine comprised of 134 million public webpages — an order of magnitude larger and significantly more intricate than ever used for this sort of research. Read more: https://ai.facebook.com/... https://twitter.com/...
(1/2) Today, we're announcing the first model capable of automatically verifying hundreds of thousands of citations at once. Read more: https://tech.fb.com/... https://twitter.com/...
